gayatri-stotram: Invokes the complete grace of Gayatri Mata
Cleanses past misdeeds and brightens the seeker's inner light
Invokes the blessing of peace and spiritual illumination
Protects the devotee from the cycle of rebirth
giridhari-stotram: Invokes the Govardhan-lila form of Lord Krishna
Calls on Krishna who lifted the hill to clear life's calamities
Ideal for Govardhan Puja and Annakut celebration
Teaches complete surrender to Krishna as the supreme protector
Traditionally recited for removal of ego and pride (as indra's was destroyed)
gita-govinda-ashtapadi-1: Praises the ten avatars Jayadeva celebrates in the opening Ashtapadi
Invokes the combined blessings of dashavatara
One of the most melodious and powerful devotional compositions
Stirs Radha-Krishna prema through Jayadeva's lyric verse
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins through remembrance of the lord's leelas
Invokes the blessing of bhakti and spiritual ecstasy
govinda-ashtakam: Deepens devotion and connection with Lord Krishna
Invokes spiritual wisdom and inner peace
Loosens the grip of worldly attachment and maya on the mind
Invokes the blessing of supreme bliss and divine grace
Shankara's eight verses settle the heart in Govinda's contemplation
grishneshwar-stotram: Equivalent to pilgrimage to Grishneshwar, the twelfth Jyotirlinga
Completes the merit of visiting all twelve Jyotirlingas
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants total purification
Bestows the spiritual accomplishments earned at this Jyotirlinga shrine
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires related to progeny and family welfare
guru-gita: Supreme scripture on Guru-tattva from Skanda Purana (Shiva-Parvati dialogue)
Contains the most sacred Guru mantras - Gurur Brahma Gurur Vishnu
Each syllable is a Mantra-Raja (king of mantras) with infinite fruit
Traditionally recited for removal of the karma-bonds accumulated over countless births
The Guru's pada-jala (foot-water) dries up the ocean of Samsara
Explains the etymology of Guru: Gu (darkness) + Ru (remover)
Grants both worldly fulfilment and final release through the Guru's grace
Essential text for Guru Purnima and daily spiritual practice
hanumad-ramayanam: Brief narrative of the complete Ramayana as sung by Hanuman
Invokes the merit of reading the entire ramayana in condensed form
Invokes the blessing of blessings of both lord rama and hanuman
Suits a short daily Rama-katha when time is limited
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and grants divine protection
Traditionally associated with peace, dharma, and prosperity
hanuman-ashtak: Classical tradition invokes this for removal of types of troubles and hardships
Invokes divine protection from negative forces
Invokes the blessing of courage and fearlessness
Sought by devotees for the granting of righteous, heartfelt wishes
Associated in tradition with chronic and difficult diseases
Traditionally associated with victory in difficult situations
hanuman-ashtottara-shatanamavali: Reciting 108 names of Hanuman bestows immense strength and courage
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears, evil spirits, and planetary afflictions
Associated in tradition with diseases and removes all forms of suffering
Used during Hanuman puja, archana, and abhishek
Best chanted on Tuesdays, Saturdays, and Hanuman Jayanti
hanuman-chalisa: Tulsidas's hymn invoked to dispel fear (bhaya-nashana) and obstacles (vighna-hara)
Calls on Hanuman's strength, courage, and clear discernment
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of earnest wishes
Invokes the blessings of Lord Rama through Hanuman's mediation
Invoked in classical tradition for protection from ailments and affliction
hanuman-gayatri: Two Hanuman Gayatri variations — Anjaneya and Ramaduta forms
Invokes extraordinary strength, courage, and valor
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of calamities and bestows fearlessness
Best chanted as japa on Tuesdays, Saturdays, and Hanuman Jayanti
Provides protection from evil forces and enemies
hanuman-hridayam: Calls on Hanuman's valor to instill courage and banish fear
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases and grants perfect health
Drives away enemies and clears persistent obstacles from life
Invokes wisdom, eloquence, and sharp intellect
Traditionally associated with the complete grace and blessings of lord rama
hanuman-mangalashtakam: Invokes the powerful auspicious blessings of Lord Hanuman
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of types of fears and grants courage
Invokes the blessing of ashta siddhi and nava nidhi through hanuman's grace
Shields devotees from evil spirits and hostile energies
Ideal for Tuesdays, Saturdays, and Hanuman Jayanti
Invokes strength, valor, and devotion to lord rama
hanuman-stavan: Invokes intelligence, strength, fame, and courage
Traditionally recited for removal of fear and bestows perfect health
Develops eloquence and sharpness of mind
Invokes Hanuman's protection as Ram Doot
Recitation fills the heart with devotion to Lord Rama
Wards off negative energies and unseen evil influences
hari-nama-mala-stotram: A garland of Vishnu's most powerful names for daily recitation
Chanting Hari's names is said to wash away the devotee's sins
Easy to memorize and use as a Japa mala
Covers all major forms and avatars of Lord Vishnu
Invokes vishnu sayujya (complete union with vishnu)
Ideal substitute when full Sahasranama is not possible
indra-gayatri: Invokes the blessings of Indra, king of the gods
Invokes the blessing of leadership qualities and authority
Vedic verse linked to timely rains and a thriving harvest
Invokes courage, strength, and victory over adversaries
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles related to power and position
indra-stuti: Invokes the mighty power of Indra for protection
Calls on the thunder-god for triumph in contests and ventures
Vedic praise tied to abundant rainfall and a fertile harvest
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles and enemies like vritra
Invokes the blessing of courage and warrior strength
Traditionally associated with wealth and material abundance
jagannath-aarti: Invokes the blessing of the merit of jagannath puri pilgrimage
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of caste distinctions through divine grace
Sung with special fervour during the Rath Yatra procession
Purifies all sins through the grace of Lord Jagannath
jagannath-ashtakam: Shankara's eight verses to Lord Jagannath of Puri
Draws the grace of Jagannath as Krishna and Vishnu combined
Invokes the Lord of Puri's shelter and auspicious presence
Chanting these stanzas is said to wash away accumulated sins
Fitting for Rath Yatra and daily Jagannath worship
jagannath-chalisa: Invokes the blessing of the merit of visiting puri dham - one of the char dhams
Frees the soul from the wheel of birth and death
Traditionally recited for removal of caste and social discrimination through jagannath's grace
Sought by devotees for both worldly needs and spiritual longing
Special significance during Rath Yatra festival
jagannath-pancharatnam: Five-jewel hymn from Skanda Purana praising Jagannath of Puri
Draws the grace of Jagannath as Krishna and Vishnu united
Calls on the Lord of Puri for shelter and prosperity
Chanting these five verses is said to dissolve past sins
Fitting for Rath Yatra and daily Jagannath worship
kalabhairava-ashtakam: Shankara's eight verses to Kala Bhairava, guardian of Kashi
Calls on Bhairava the dispeller of fear in his fierce form
Bestows Bhairava's shelter and removal of the dread of death
Chanting these stanzas is said to wash away one's sins
Fitting for Kala Bhairava Ashtami and daily worship
kali-aarti: Traditionally recited for removal of all fears, evil forces, and negative energies
Provides fierce protection to devotees in times of crisis
Essential aarti for Kali Puja on Deepawali night
Kindles courage and fearlessness in the heart of the devotee
Invokes the most powerful form of the Divine Mother
kali-gayatri: Invokes the fierce protective energy of Goddess Kali
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears and grants fearlessness
Scatters enemies and the dark forces that trouble the seeker
Powerful for tantric sadhana and spiritual awakening
Invokes mahakali's grace when chanted with devotion
kamadeva-stotram: Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires for marriage, love, and marital happiness
Bestows beauty, charm, and magnetic appeal on the devotee
Contains Kamadeva Gayatri for invoking the deity of love
Invokes the blessing of saubhagya (marital bliss and good fortune)
Especially powerful when recited in Vasant Ritu (spring)
Invokes the creative energy responsible for worldly creation
